Defendants State Of California And Kamala Harris' Motion To Strike 2nd Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Nov-10-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Defendants State Of California And Kamala Harris' Motion To Strike portions of 2nd Amended Complaint ( Page 2, paragraph 5, lines 13-17; Page 7, paragraph 21, lines 5-10) is granted with 10 days leave to amend. No opposition filed. Order signed. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR # 5976

Defendants State Of California, Kamala Harris' Demurrer To 2nd Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Nov-10-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Joinder of Defendants State Of California and Kamala Harris' to Defendant CCSF Demurrer To 2nd Amended Complaint is sustained. As a threshold matter, Plaintiff has standing to bring this action under CCP section 526a. The Complaint alleges that she has paid taxes within the requisite timeframe and seeks to prevent the illegal expenditure of funds by officers and agents of the state, city, and county. (See Wirin v. Horrall (1948) 85 Cal.App.2d 497 [holding that the mere "expending [of] the time of the paid police officers of the city of Los Angeles in performing illegal and unauthorized acts" constituted an unlawful use of funds which could be enjoined under 526a].) As to the first cause of action, Plaintiff fails to state a claim upon which relief may be granted. The procedures allegedly followed by Defendants in providing the notices of forfeiture proceedings conform to the requirements of Health & Safety Code section 11488.4. The procedures also comply with Cuevas v. Sup. Ct. (2013) 221 Cal.App.4th 1312, which mandates that either the Attorney General or the district attorney make the forfeiture decision, as the Complaint alleges that police officers consult with the district attorney concerning the facts of the matter, and obtain authorization prior to providing the notices. Thus, the decision to proceed with forfeiture is allegedly and legally made by the district attorney. Furthermore, neither the statute nor Cuevas require the district attorney to personally draft, sign, or deliver the notices. Moreover, Health & Safety Code section 11488.2, as applicable here, requires only that the seizing police officer either refer the matter in writing or return the property. The statute does not limit the authority of the district attorney to make the forfeiture decision based upon any such written referral. Finally, the Complaint does not allege that the form of the notices fails to conform to the statutory requirements. Regarding the notices provided prior to the change in practices, the Complaint fails to seek any remedy available to Plaintiff. As to the second cause of action against all Defendants and to all causes of action against Defendant Kamala Harris, the Demurrer is moot in light of Plaintiff's Request for Dismissal filed on August 27, 2014. Plaintiff has 10 days leave to amend. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/eg

Defendants City And County Of San Francisco, San Francisco Police Department, San Francisco County District Attorney'S Office, George Gascon And San Francisco Sheriff'S Department'S Demurrer To 2nd Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Nov-10-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Defendants City And County Of San Francisco, San Francisco Police Department, San Francisco County District Attorney's Office, George Gascon And San Francisco Sheriff's Department's Demurrer To 2nd Amended Complaint is sustained. As a threshold matter, Plaintiff has standing to bring this action under CCP section 526a. The Complaint alleges that she has paid taxes within the requisite timeframe and seeks to prevent the illegal expenditure of funds by officers and agents of the state, city, and county. (See Wirin v. Horrall (1948) 85 Cal.App.2d 497 [holding that the mere "expending [of] the time of the paid police officers of the city of Los Angeles in performing illegal and unauthorized acts" constituted an unlawful use of funds which could be enjoined under 526a].) As to the first cause of action, Plaintiff fails to state a claim upon which relief may be granted. The procedures allegedly followed by Defendants in providing the notices of forfeiture proceedings conform to the requirements of Health & Safety Code section 11488.4. The procedures also comply with Cuevas v. Sup. Ct. (2013) 221 Cal.App.4th 1312, which mandates that either the Attorney General or the district attorney make the forfeiture decision, as the Complaint alleges that police officers consult with the district attorney concerning the facts of the matter, and obtain authorization prior to providing the notices. Thus, the decision to proceed with forfeiture is allegedly and legally made by the district attorney. Furthermore, neither the statute nor Cuevas require the district attorney to personally draft, sign, or deliver the notices. Moreover, Health & Safety Code section 11488.2, as applicable here, requires only that the seizing police officer either refer the matter in writing or return the property. The statute does not limit the authority of the district attorney to make the forfeiture decision based upon any such written referral. Finally, the Complaint does not allege that the form of the notices fails to conform to the statutory requirements. Regarding the notices provided prior to the change in practices, the Complaint fails to seek any remedy available to Plaintiff. As to the second cause of action against all Defendants and to all causes of action against Defendant Kamala Harris, the Demurrer is moot in light of Plaintiff's Request for Dismissal filed on August 27, 2014. Plaintiff has 10 days leave to amend. Order signed. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/eg
